verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is canonically a secreted serine protease produced by prostate epithelial cells, with no transmembrane domain — it is secreted into prostatic fluid and circulates in blood. However, a 2025 Clinical Cancer Research study (Shen et al.) directly confirmed cell-surface expression of KLK2 on prostate cancer cell lines (VCaP, confirmed by FACS and confocal co-localization with EpCAM) and in freshly dissociated patient-derived mCRPC tumor cells. This surface display is restricted to AR+ prostate cancer/prostate lineage cells and is not documented in normal non-prostatic tissues. The mechanism of surface retention is not a conventional TM anchor; the protein is still a secreted protease whose surface pool is documented only in the oncogenic/prostate-lineage context. Multiple clinical-stage surface-directed therapeutics (KLK2×CD3 bispecific Pasritamig, 225Ac-radioligand JNJ-69086420, CAR-T) further validate surface accessibility in this cancer context. This maps to contextual/cell_state_induced (oncogenic transformation / tissue-restricted surface display in AR+ prostate cancer cells). The surface pool is not present at baseline in normal tissues, and the anchoring mechanism remains uncertain (possibly reversible non-covalent tethering to a surface partner in cancer cells).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Molecular mechanism of KLK2 surface retention not yet defined — unclear whether it is a stable/wash-resistant attachment to a TM partner or a reversible association; also unclear whether normal prostate (non-cancer) cells show any surface pool.
